1.1: We evangelize to you that which was from the beginning, that which we have heard and we have seen, that which we have perceived with our eyes and we have touched with our hands- him who is The Word of Life.
1.2: And The Life was revealed and we perceived and we testify and we proclaim to you the Life Eternal, that which was with The Father and was revealed to us.
1.3: And the thing which we have seen and heard we show also to you, that you will have communion with us, and our communion is with The Father and with his Son Yeshua The Messiah.
1.4: And these things we write to you that the joy which we have in you may be complete.
1.5: And this is The Good News that we have heard from him and we evangelize to you: God is Light, and there is no darkness at all in him.
1.6: And if we say that we have communion with him and we walk in darkness, we are lying, and we are not informed of the truth.
1.7: But if we walk in The Light as he is in The Light, we have communion with each other and the blood of Yeshua his Son purges us from all of our sins.
1.8: And if we shall say we do not have sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
1.9: But if we confess our sins, He is faithful and righteous to forgive us our sins, and he will purge us from all our evil.
1.10: And if we say that we have not sinned, we make him a liar and his word is not with us.

2.1: Children, I write these things to you so that you will not sin, and if a person will sin, we have “The Redeemer of the accursed” with The Father - Yeshua The Messiah, The Righteous One.
2.2: For He is The Atonement who is for the sake of our sins, and not in our place only, but also in the place of the entire universe.
2.3: And by this we sense that we know him, if we keep his commandments.
2.4: For he who says, “I know him”, and does not keep his commandments, is lying and the truth is not in him.
2.5: But he who keeps his word, in this one truly the love of God is perfected, for by this we know that we are in him.
2.6: He who says, “I am in him”, must walk according to his walk.
2.7: Beloved ones, I am not writing a new commandment to you, but an ancient commandment which you had from the beginning; but the ancient commandment is that word which you have heard.
2.8: Again, a new commandment I am writing to you, that which is true in him and in you, for the darkness has passed and the true light begins to appear.
2.9: Whoever says, therefore, that he is in the light and hates his brother, is in darkness still.
2.10: But he who loves his brother dwells in light and there is no violation in him.
2.11: But he who hates his brother is in darkness and walks in darkness and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
2.12: I write to you, children, that your sins have been forgiven you because of his Name.
2.13: I write to you, fathers, that you have known him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, that you have conquered The Evil One. I write to you children, that you have known The Father.
2.14: I have written to you, fathers, that you have known him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, that you are strong, and the word of God dwells in you and you have conquered The Evil One.
2.15: Do not love the world, neither the things that are in it, for whoever loves the world does not have the love of The Father in him.
2.16: For everything that is in the world: the desire of the body and the lust of the eyes and the pride of temporal life, these are not from The Father but these are from the world.
2.17: The world is passing and its lust, but he who does the will of God continues for eternity.
2.18: My children, it is an end time, and according to what you have heard that The False Messiah comes, already there have been many false messiahs, and by this we know that it is an end time.
2.19: They went out from us but they were not of us, for if they had been of us, they would have remained; but they went out from us to disclose that they were not of us.
2.20: And you have an anointing from The Holy One, and you discern every person.
2.21: I have not written to you because you do not know the truth, but because you do know it, and that no lie is from the truth.
2.22: Who is a liar but whoever denies that Yeshua is The Messiah? Such a person is a false messiah. He who denies The Father denies also the Son.
2.23: He who denies The Son also does not believe in The Father; whoever confesses The Son confesses The Father also.
2.24: And anything which you have heard from the first, let remain with you, for if that which you have heard from the first remains with you, you are remaining in The Father and in The Son.
2.25: And this is The Promise which he has promised us: Eternal Life!
2.26: But I have written these things to you because of those who seduce you.
2.27: And if The Anointing will remain with you which you have received from him, you do not need anyone to teach you, but as The Anointing who is from God is teaching you about everything, and is true, and there is no falsehood in him, even as he has taught you, abide in him.
2.28: And now my children, abide in him, that when he is revealed, we shall not be ashamed before him, but we shall have boldness at his arrival.
2.29: And if you know that he is righteous, know also that everyone who does righteousness is from him.

3.1: And behold how much the love of The Father abounds to us, that he has called us and also has made us children; because of this, the world does not know us, because it does not know him.
3.2: Beloved, now we are the children of God, and it has not been revealed until now what we are going to be, but we know that when he has been revealed, we shall be in his likeness, and we shall see him just as what he is.
3.3: And everyone who has this hope upon him purifies himself, just as he is pure.
3.4: But whoever commits sin commits evil, for sin is entirely evil.
3.5: And you know that he was revealed to take away our sins and there is no sin in him.
3.6: No one who remains in him commits sin, and no one who sins has seen him, neither has he known him.
3.7: Children, let no one deceive you; he who does righteousness is righteous even as The Messiah also is righteous.
3.8: He who commits sin is from Satan, because Satan is a sinner from the beginning. The Son of God appeared for this reason: To destroy the works of Satan.
3.9: No one who is begotten from God commits sin, because his seed is in him and he is not able to sin because he has been begotten from God.
3.10: By this the children of God are distinguished from the children of Satan: No one who does not do righteousness, neither loves his brother, is from God.
3.11: For this is the commandment that you have heard from the first: “You shall love one another”,
3.12: Not like Cain who was from The Evil One and murdered his brother; and why did he murder him? Only because his works were evil and those of his brother were righteous.
3.13: Do not be surprised, my brethren, if the world hates you.
3.14: We know that we have departed from death into life by this: Because we love the brethren. He who does not love his brother remains in death.
3.15: For everyone who hates his brother murders a person, and you know that eternal life cannot abide in anyone who murders a person.
3.16: By this we know his love toward us, for he gave his Life in our place, and it is right for us that we would give our lives for the sake of our brothers.
3.17: Whoever has worldly possessions and sees his brother who has a need and withholds his compassion from him, how is the love of God in him?
3.18: Children, let us not love one another with words and with speech, but in deeds and in truth.
3.19: And by this we are made known that we are from the truth, and we assure our hearts before he comes.
3.20: For if our heart condemns us, how much greater is God than our heart? And he knows all things.
3.21: Beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we have boldness before God.
3.22: And we shall receive all the things that we ask from him, because we keep his commandments and we do good before him.
3.23: And this is his commandment: that we believe in The Name of his Son, Yeshua The Messiah, and we should love one another, as he commanded us.
3.24: And whoever keeps his commandments is kept by him and he dwells in him, and by this we perceive that he lodges in us, from his Spirit, that one whom he has given to us.

4.1: Beloved, do not believe all spirits, but be distinguishing between the spirits whether they are from God, because many false Prophets have gone out into the world.
4.2: The Spirit of God is known by this: every spirit who confesses that Yeshua The Messiah has come in the flesh is from God.
4.3: And no spirit that does not confess that Yeshua has come in the flesh is from God, but this is from that False Messiah, him whom you have heard that he will come, and already he is in the world.
4.4: You are from God, children, and you have conquered them, because he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world.
4.5: And these are from the world, therefore they speak from the world and the world listens to them.
4.6: But we are from God and he who knows God listens to us and he who is not from God does not listen to us; by this we perceive The Spirit of Truth and the spirit of deception.
4.7: Beloved, let us love one another, because love is from God, and everyone who loves is born of God and knows God,
4.8: Because God is love, and everyone who does not love does not know God.
4.9: In this the love of God is known to us: for God sent his only Son to the world so that it shall live by him.
4.10: In this is love: it was not that we loved God, but he has loved us, and he sent us his Son, the atonement for the sake of our sins.
4.11: Beloved, if God loves us in this way, we are indebted also to love one another.
4.12: No person has ever seen God, but if we love one another, God abides in us, and his love is fulfilled in us.
4.13: In this we know that we abide in him and he abides in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.
4.14: And we see and do testify that The Father has sent his Son, The Savior for the world.
4.15: And everyone who confesses Yeshua, that he is The Son of God, God dwells in him and he dwells in God.
4.16: And we believe and we know the love that God has for us, for God is love, and everyone who dwells in love dwells in God.
4.17: By this, love is fulfilled with us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because as he is, so also are we, in this world.
4.18: There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear, because fear is by suspicion, but he who fears is not grown up in love.
4.19: Therefore we shall love God because he first loved us.
4.20: But if a man will say, “I love God”, and he hates his brother, he is lying; for he who does not love his brother who is visible, how is it possible for him to love God who is invisible?
4.21: And we have received this commandment from him: “Everyone who loves God shall love his brother also.”

5.1: Everyone who believes that Yeshua is The Messiah is born from God, and everyone who loves him who begot, loves also the one who is begotten of him.
5.2: And by this we know that we love the children of God, whenever we love God and we do his commandments.
5.3: For this is the love of God: to keep his commandments; and his commandments are not burdensome.
5.4: Because everyone who is born from God conquers the universe; and this is the victory that conquers the universe - our faith.
5.5: Who is he who conquers the universe but he who believes that Yeshua is The Son of God?
5.6: This is he who came by water and blood, Yeshua The Messiah; it was not by water only, but by water and blood.
5.7: And The Spirit testifies because The Spirit is the truth.
5.8: And there are three testifying: The Spirit and the water and the blood, and the three of them are in one.
5.9: If we receive the testimony of men, how much greater is the testimony of God? And this is the testimony of God which he testifies about his Son:
5.10: Everyone who believes in The Son of God has this witness in his soul. Everyone who does not believe God makes him a liar because he does not believe the testimony that God testifies concerning his Son.
5.11: And this is the testimony: God has given us Eternal Life, and that life is in his Son.
5.12: Everyone who lays hold of The Son lays hold also of The Life, and everyone who does not lay hold of The Son of God does not have The Life in him.
5.13: These things I have written to you that you will know that you have Eternal Life, you who believe in the name of The Son of God.
5.14: And this is the confidence which we have toward him: Everything that we ask him according to his will, he hears for us.
5.15: And if we are convinced that he hears us concerning whatever we ask from him, we trust that even now we receive the things that we desire which we ask from him.
5.16: If a man sees his brother who sins a sin that is not worthy of death, let him ask, and Life will be given to him for those who are not sinning unto death; for there is mortal sin; I do not say that a man should pray for this.
5.17: For every evil is sin, and there is sin that is not mortal.
5.18: And we know that no one who is born from God commits sin, for he who is born from God keeps his soul and The Evil One does not touch it.
5.19: We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in The Evil One.
5.20: And we know that The Son of God has come and he has given us a mind to know The True One and to be in The True One- in his Son, Yeshua The Messiah. This One is The True God and The Life Eternal.
5.21: My children, keep yourselves from the worship of idols.
